title: Fog of War Update Cycle
entry_point: Logic.update() calls fogControl.update() each game tick when state.rules.fog == true
activity_flow: [Guard] staticFog && !loadedStatic → pushStaticBlocks(false) + updateStatic() (sync, main thread) → loadedStatic=true
[Thread init] staticFogThread == null? → spawn StaticFogThread (daemon, NORM_PRIORITY-1)
[Thread init] dynamicFogThread == null? → spawn DynamicFogThread (daemon, NORM_PRIORITY-1)
Compute iterated = max(1, size / min(5, size)) — iterate ~20% of buildings per tick
updateFogVisibility(): if !wasVisible && !inFogTo(player.team()) → mark visible, update minimap shadow
For each non-AI team in state.teams.present:
Get or create FogData for team
YES → pushEvent(event, skipRender=false): add to staticEvents, send to renderer.fog if player's team
YES → add all hasFogRadius buildings for team → dynamicEventQueue (FogEvent per build)
[Guard] justLoaded? → updateDynamic(new Bits(256)) inline; justLoaded=false  (force no pop-in)
static_fog_thread_flow: For each event: decode (x, y, radius, team); get FogData
circle(data.staticData, x, y, radius): Bresenham circle → hline() sets Bits in staticData
Result: staticData Bits permanently set (explored tiles never re-concealed)
dynamic_fog_thread_flow: For each event: decode (x, y, radius+1, team)
If team not yet in cleared: data.write.clear() (erase previous frame visibility)
circle(data.write, x, y, radius+1): draw new visibility circles
For each team in cleared: swap data.read ↔ data.write
Result: data.read now reflects current dynamic visibility; data.write ready for next frame
async_core_relationship: AsyncCore.begin(): calls p.begin() for all AsyncProcess, submits p.process() to thread pool
AsyncCore.end(): awaits all futures, calls p.end() for all AsyncProcess
PhysicsProcess: begin() syncs unit positions into PhysicsBody refs; process() runs PhysicsWorld.update() (collision resolution) on async thread; end() moves entities by delta
NOTE: FogControl is NOT an AsyncProcess — it runs synchronously in Logic.update(), separate from AsyncCore pipeline
key_data_structures: FogData.staticData: Bits[ww*wh] — permanently revealed tiles (written by StaticFogThread)
FogData.read: Bits[ww*wh] — current dynamic visibility, read by game logic (isVisible calls)
FogData.write: Bits[ww*wh] — scratch buffer redrawn each dynamic update cycle
FogData.dynamicUpdated: dirty flag set by unit move / tile change / unit death events
FogData.lastDynamicMs: throttle timestamp, dynamic updates capped at 25 FPS (40ms interval)
staticEvents: LongSeq — packed FogEvent longs (x:16, y:16, radius:16, team:8) for static updates
dynamicEvents: LongSeq (sync) — packed FogEvent longs for current dynamic update pass
dynamicEventQueue: LongSeq — staging area, flushed to dynamicEvents under lock
event_triggers: ResetEvent → stop(): interrupt both threads, null fog array
TileChangeEvent (build with hasFogRadius) → pushEvent to staticEvents + data.dynamicUpdated=true
forceUpdate(team, build) called by Building (e.g. Turret, Radar) → dynamicUpdated=true + push static event
reveal_conceal_logic: REVEAL (static): circle() paints hlines into staticData Bits — permanent, never cleared
REVEAL (dynamic): circle() paints hlines into write buffer; buffer swap makes it live
CONCEAL (dynamic): write buffer is fully cleared before redrawing each update cycle — tiles not covered by any current unit/building fog radius are automatically concealed
CONCEAL (static): never — static data is append-only
Visibility query: isVisible(team, x, y) reads data.read.get(pos); isDiscovered reads staticData.get(pos)
concurrency_notes: staticEvents: synchronized on its own object for main↔StaticFogThread access
dynamicEvents: synchronized on its own object for main↔DynamicFogThread access
notifyStatic / notifyDynamic: separate monitor objects for waking threads
fog array: volatile reference; FogData.read/write: volatile Bits fields
Comment in code: dynamic thread notify WILL block until rendering done — known bottleneck
PhysicsProcess runs on separate AsyncLogic-Thread pool, fully independent of fog threads
